title: Product Manager, Xbox Storefront (Commerce)
bullets: Own third-party subscription integrations and monetization experiences across Console, PC App, Xbox.com, and Mobile surfaces.
Led end-to-end integration of Fortnite Crew and Ubisoft+ Classics into Game Pass Ultimate, enabling bundled monetization pathways across four client surfaces.
Drove launch of Ubisoft+ Classics as a standalone subscription, delivering against fixed contractual milestones through structured partner alignment and cross-functional execution.
Partner directly with Ubisoft in recurring syncs to define launch scope, resolve tradeoffs, and align on revenue expectations.

Defined cross-system identity and entitlement architecture governing activation, account linking, renewal, revocation, and multi-device access; reduced inconsistent identity states and purchase ambiguity.
Authored PRDs and UX flows enabling seamless third-party subscription activation and lifecycle management within Xbox commerce systems.

Audited the third-party subscription and commerce space, then authored integration principles (entitlement standards, UX parity, lifecycle transparency, revenue visibility) to align roadmap prioritization and partner onboarding.
Drove roadmap prioritization across merchandising, commerce, and platform services to meet fixed timelines while maintaining UX consistency.
Built launch readiness framework coordinating engineering delivery, QA validation, GTM planning, and merchandising strategy to de-risk multi-surface releases.
Added an experimentation skill to the team’s shared AI system repository used with GitHub Copilot, making it easier to run structured product experiments and reuse trusted prompts and workflows.
Run a recurring v-team forum spanning nine organizations, convening internal teams, intern cohorts, and cross-org partners to align on priorities, dependencies, and shared outcomes.
Represent team work in membership ship rooms—distilling execution status, tradeoffs, and customer impact into clear narratives for senior leadership decision-making.

Built a GPT-powered onboarding assistant for new PMs and interns after observing repeated ramp-up friction across the team; validated with hiring managers and incoming hires, who cited faster time-to-productivity, easier documentation discovery, and clearer role expectations.
Mentor PM intern through feature scoping and cross-functional execution, strengthening product thinking and stakeholder alignment.

Interview PM intern candidates and facilitate working sessions with C-suite executives to shape intern programming and culture—translating leadership priorities into a cohesive cohort experience, clear expectations, and norms that emphasize learning, psychological safety, and cross-functional collaboration.

title: Product Manager Intern — Xbox Storefront (Commerce)
bullets: Contributed to Xbox Offer Wallet (promotions platform): improved UX and notifications vs. legacy XMF; sustained redemption rates ~2× legacy with a slower post-launch decay curve. Partnered across commerce, identity, platform services, and merchandising on success metrics, MVP scope, and fraud-safe redemption/processing flows.

Supported scaled campaign execution: 15+ campaigns in-cycle vs. 8 in full FY23; first waves reached 500K+ users not targetable under prior tooling ($3M in offers); cycle targeting 6.5M+ users and $62M+ in offer value; projected $76M gross revenue ($69M net) vs. $37M gross for the entire prior fiscal year.

title: Explore Intern (PM + SWE)
bullets: Built PDP features using C#, TypeScript, and React across Xbox console storefront; developed foundational understanding of large-scale commerce services, entitlement systems, and identity architecture.
Rotational exposure to PM and SWE work across front- and back-end development in a large-scale product org.

title: Student Researcher
bullets: Research with Christine Bassem on time-series forecasting for ride demand using public NYC taxi data; experimented with LSTM and Neural Prophet models to improve prediction quality.

title: Student Mentor (TA)
bullets: Section lead for Engineering Leadership Lab (6.9110); manage other GEL 2s (mentors) and set vision for the section.
Give feedback to cohort and mentor peers to develop leadership skills.

company: Wellesley College — HHMI Diversity & Inclusion (CS Department)
title: Intern
bullets: Built an inclusive community supporting minority students in the department; ran community events with faculty.
Tutored CS 230 (Data Structures) students.
Gave campus tours to faculty candidates and supported the hiring process.

title: Secretary / Treasurer / Senator
bullets: Managed society finances in Excel; secured college funding approvals, ran fundraising, and recorded meeting minutes.

title: Senator / Lecture Chair
bullets: Mediated discussion in a diverse community; advocated for constituents in student government.
Recruited academics to lead lectures on topics the community wanted to explore.

title: Co-founder
bullets: Conceived a student productivity app for remote learning motivation and schoolwork organization; built prototypes in InVision.
Project site: https://remarkable-superb-titanium.glitch.me

title: Founder & Tutor
bullets: Designed curriculum and taught PowerPoint, Word, and digital privacy to 200+ middle-school girls from under-resourced schools in Lahore and home village.
Project site: https://erasingdigitalilliteracy.ouasite.com

company: Wellesley College — Class of 2025 Alumni Council
title: Council Member
bullets: Plan reunion and class-wide events; gather class updates from peers and produce newsletters that keep the cohort informed and engaged.

title: Board Member; Young Alumnae Chair & Communications Chair
bullets: Serve on the board as Young Alumnae Chair and Communications Chair (from Mar 2026): shape regional programming, early-career alum outreach, and consistent club communications across channels.
